title: Anthropic Approved 1.5 Billion Dollar Copyright Settlement Agreement, Will Pay Compensation for 500,000 Works
excerpt: According to Reuters, the U.S. federal court officially approved a $1.5 billion class-action settlement between Anthropic and authors and publishers on Monday.
content_html:
According to Reuters, the U.S. federal court officially approved a $1.5 billion class-action settlement between Anthropic and authors and publishers on Monday. The agreement is intended to resolve litigation against Anthropic over alleged copyright infringement during the training of its AI models, and the company will begin paying compensation to the relevant copyright holders.

Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California signed the final approval document. Previously, retired judge William Alsup had provisionally approved the settlement and ruled that Anthropic had illegally downloaded and stored millions of copyrighted books.

Under the compensation plan, each affected work is expected to receive $3,000 in damages, involving approximately 500,000 works, with the amount to be shared among the authors and publishers who hold the copyrights. This sum is considered one of the largest settlements in the field of U.S. copyright law.

However, the agreement does not fully resolve the legal disputes surrounding AI training data. Judge Alsup previously supported Anthropic on the core issue of the case, stating that using copyrighted texts to train AI models may fall under "fair use," which was seen as an important development in AI copyright cases. At the same time, he pointed out that some of the ways Anthropic obtained training data were illegal.

Anthropic had primarily obtained training data through two methods: some from books legally purchased and scanned, and others from pirated websites such as Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror. The court deemed the latter method as illegal. To avoid further trials and potential high penalties, Anthropic ultimately chose to reach a settlement.

Industry experts note that while this settlement ends the case, it has not established a unified legal standard for the entire AI industry. Because Anthropic chose to settle, the case did not proceed to an appeal, and Judge Alsup's previous ruling on "fair use" in AI training will not become a binding judicial precedent.

Currently, lawsuits over the copyright of training data for AI models are still ongoing, including cases against companies such as Google, Meta, Midjourney, and OpenAI. Recently, publishing houses such as Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, author Scott Turow, and SCRIBE also jointly filed a lawsuit against Google, accusing it of using copyrighted works without permission to train the Gemini AI platform.

As generative AI continues to develop rapidly, how to balance the needs of model training with copyright protection has become a major legal and business challenge for the global AI industry. While the Anthropic settlement provides one solution path, the long-term rules for AI data compliance in the industry remain to be further clarified.

blocks.html_en: According to Reuters, the U.S. federal court officially approved a $1.5 billion class-action settlement between Anthropic and authors and publishers on Monday. sentences.en: According to Reuters, the U.S. federal court officially approved a $1.5 billion class-action settlement between Anthropic and authors and publishers on Monday.
sentences.zh: 据路透社报道，美国联邦法院周一正式批准了 Anthropic 与作家和出版商之间达成的 15 亿美元集体诉讼和解协议。

sentences.en: The agreement is intended to resolve litigation against Anthropic over alleged copyright infringement during the training of its AI models, and the company will begin paying compensation to the relevant copyright holders.

sentences.en: Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California signed the final approval document.

Recently, publishing houses such as Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, author Scott Turow, and SCRIBE also jointly filed a lawsuit against Google, accusing it of using copyrighted works without permission to train the Gemini AI platform.

sentences.en: Recently, publishing houses such as Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, author Scott Turow, and SCRIBE also jointly filed a lawsuit against Google, accusing it of using copyrighted works without permission to train the Gemini AI platform.
sentences.zh: 近日，阿歇特、圣智、爱思唯尔、作家斯科特·图罗、SCRIBE等出版社也联合对谷歌提起诉讼，指控其未经许可使用版权作品来训练Gemini AI平台。

blocks.html_en: As generative AI continues to develop rapidly, how to balance the needs of model training with copyright protection has become a major legal and business challenge for the global AI industry. While the Anthropic settlement provides one solution path, the long-term rules for AI data compliance in the industry remain to be further clarified.

sentences.en: As generative AI continues to develop rapidly, how to balance the needs of model training with copyright protection has become a major legal and business challenge for the global AI industry.
sentences.zh: 随着生成式人工智能的持续快速发展，如何平衡模型训练的需求与版权保护已成为全球人工智能行业面临的重大法律和商业挑战。

sentences.en: While the Anthropic settlement provides one solution path, the long-term rules for AI data compliance in the industry remain to be further clarified.
sentences.zh: 虽然 Anthropic 和解协议提供了一种解决方案路径，但行业内人工智能数据合规的长期规则仍有待进一步明确。

content_html_zh:
据路透社报道，美国联邦法院周一正式批准了 Anthropic 与作家和出版商之间达成的 15 亿美元集体诉讼和解协议。 该协议旨在解决针对 Anthropic 在人工智能模型训练过程中涉嫌侵犯版权的诉讼，该公司将开始向相关版权所有者支付赔偿。

美国加州北区地方法院法官 Araceli Martinez-Olguin 签署了最终批准文件。 此前，退休法官 William Alsup 暂时批准了和解协议，并裁定 Anthropic 非法下载和存储了数百万本受版权保护的书籍。

根据赔偿计划，每件受影响的作品预计将获得3000美元的赔偿，涉及约50万件作品，赔偿金额将由拥有版权的作者和出版商分摊。 这笔金额被认为是美国版权法领域最大的和解金额之一。

然而，该协议并没有完全解决围绕人工智能训练数据的法律纠纷。 Alsup 法官此前在该案的核心问题上支持 Anthropic，称使用受版权保护的文本来训练 AI 模型可能属于“合理使用”，这被视为 AI 版权案件的重要进展。 同时他指出，Anthropic获取训练数据的一些方式是非法的。

Anthropic主要通过两种方式获取训练数据：一部分是通过合法购买并扫描的书籍获取的，一部分是通过Library Genesis、Pirate Library Mirror等盗版网站获取的。 法院认为后一种方法非法。 为了避免进一步的审判和潜在的高额处罚，Anthropic 最终选择达成和解。

业内专家指出，此次和解虽然结束了案件，但并没有为整个人工智能行业建立统一的法律标准。 由于Anthropic选择和解，案件并未进入上诉阶段，阿尔苏普法官此前关于人工智能训练中“合理使用”的裁决也不会成为具有约束力的司法先例。

目前，有关AI模型训练数据版权的诉讼仍在继续，其中包括针对Google、Meta、Midjourney和OpenAI等公司的案件。 近日，阿歇特、圣智、爱思唯尔、作家斯科特·图罗、SCRIBE等出版社也联合对谷歌提起诉讼，指控其未经许可使用版权作品来训练Gemini AI平台。

随着生成式人工智能的持续快速发展，如何平衡模型训练的需求与版权保护已成为全球人工智能行业面临的重大法律和商业挑战。 虽然 Anthropic 和解协议提供了一种解决方案路径，但行业内人工智能数据合规的长期规则仍有待进一步明确。
